project.name: grouply
project.description: A collaborative project management application with AI-powered task management, real-time React Flow graph visualization, RAG-based document search, and Supabase backend.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js server-rendered page for the project workspace that authenticates the user, verifies membership, fetches all project data (sections, tasks, members, checklist items), and renders the WorkspaceClient component.

nodes.name: WorkspacePage
nodes.summary: Server component that loads all project data from Supabase, guards against unauthenticated or unauthorized access, builds the AI context, and passes everything to WorkspaceClient.

nodes.name: WorkspaceClient.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side root component that wraps the workspace in a data-loading provider (WorkspaceData) and renders the full layout (WorkspaceLayout), bridging server-fetched props with client-side interactivity.

nodes.name: WorkspaceClient
nodes.summary: Composes WorkspaceData and WorkspaceLayout to create the interactive client workspace, passing server-fetched initial data down to child components.

nodes.name: ChecklistSidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Collapsible sidebar component that displays project checklist items with real-time Supabase subscriptions, supports item highlighting tied to graph nodes, and allows adding new checklist items.

nodes.name: ChecklistSidebar
nodes.summary: Main sidebar component with real-time task and checklist updates via Supabase channels, collapsible state persisted to localStorage, and interactive item-to-graph-node highlighting.

nodes.name: ContributionBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Component that visualizes per-member task contribution percentages as a horizontal bar, loading done/in-progress task counts from Supabase with real-time updates.

nodes.name: ContributionBar
nodes.summary: Renders a collapsible contribution bar showing each member's share of completed and in-progress tasks, with live Supabase updates and avatar display.

nodes.name: DocumentsTab.tsx
nodes.summary: Tab component for managing project documents, supporting drag-and-drop file uploads, AI-powered document analysis, and document deletion against the project documents API.

nodes.name: DocumentsTab
nodes.summary: Provides a drag-and-drop or click-to-upload interface for project documents, with analyze and delete actions calling backend API routes, and displays the document list with status indicators.

nodes.name: GraphToolbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Toolbar component for the React Flow task graph that provides auto-layout via Dagre, fit-to-view, and view-toggle controls.

nodes.name: applyDagreLayout
nodes.summary: Computes an automatic top-down Dagre layout for the given React Flow nodes and edges, returning repositioned node objects.

nodes.name: GraphToolbar
nodes.summary: Renders toolbar buttons for auto-layout, fit view, and view switching, integrating with the React Flow context and graph Zustand store.

nodes.name: TaskGraph.tsx
nodes.summary: React Flow canvas component rendering tasks and sections as interactive nodes with real-time Supabase sync, supporting drag-to-reposition, dependency edge creation, double-click task creation, and task drawer opening.

nodes.name: TaskGraph
nodes.summary: Full-featured React Flow graph with live data subscriptions, node drag persistence to Supabase, connect-to-set-dependency, and double-click-to-create-task interactions.

nodes.name: DependencyEdge.tsx
nodes.summary: Custom React Flow edge component representing a task dependency, rendering a bezier path with a hover tooltip and a remove button that clears the blocked_by_id in Supabase.

nodes.name: DependencyEdge
nodes.summary: Renders a dependency arrow between two task nodes with an interactive tooltip showing the blocked task name and a click-to-remove button that updates Supabase.

nodes.name: GhostTaskNode.tsx
nodes.summary: Lightweight React Flow node component that renders a translucent placeholder for a task not yet created, used as a visual hint in the graph.

nodes.name: SectionNode.tsx
nodes.summary: React Flow node component for a section container that supports inline renaming with Supabase persistence and visual selection highlighting.

nodes.name: SectionNode
nodes.summary: Renders a labeled section container node with double-click-to-edit renaming that saves to Supabase and fires an onUpdated callback.

nodes.name: TaskNode.tsx
nodes.summary: React Flow node component for a task card that displays status, assignee avatar, and name, with click-to-cycle-status behavior persisted to Supabase.

nodes.name: InviteButton.tsx
nodes.summary: Button component that generates a project invite token in Supabase, copies the resulting invite link to the clipboard, and shows a toast notification.

nodes.name: InviteButton
nodes.summary: Handles invite link generation by inserting a new invite record, then copies the shareable URL to the clipboard with loading state management.

nodes.name: SectionAccordion.tsx
nodes.summary: Accordion component that groups tasks under a section header and can be expanded or collapsed, rendering each task as a TaskRow.

nodes.name: SectionAccordion
nodes.summary: Renders a collapsible section header with a task count badge and a list of TaskRow children, toggling open state on header click.

nodes.name: ActivityLog.tsx
nodes.summary: Displays a chronological list of task history entries with actor name, action description, and relative timestamp using date-fns.

nodes.name: ActivityLog
nodes.summary: Renders each task history entry with the actor's name, a description of the change, and a human-readable relative time.

nodes.name: ClaimBadge.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive badge component showing users who have claimed a task, with real-time Supabase updates and click-to-claim/unclaim functionality for the current user.

nodes.name: ClaimBadge
nodes.summary: Renders avatar stacks of claimers with a live subscription, toggles the current user's claim on click, and respects the task's existing assignee.

nodes.name: StatusBadge.tsx
nodes.summary: Presentational badge component that maps a task status value to a color-coded label, accepting an optional click handler for cycling.

nodes.name: StatusBadge
nodes.summary: Renders a styled status label with color variant based on the task status string and calls onClick when clicked.

nodes.name: TaskDrawer.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured slide-out drawer for editing a task's name, description, deadline, status, assignee, type, checklist link, linked documents, and optional flag, with activity log, claim list, and AI-ask integration.

nodes.name: TaskDrawer
nodes.summary: Manages all task editing interactions in a sheet overlay, writing changes to Supabase, recording history entries, and supporting AI question delegation via onAskAI.

nodes.name: TaskList.tsx
nodes.summary: List view of project tasks organized by section with status filter chips, real-time Supabase reload, and inline create-task dialog.

nodes.name: TaskList
nodes.summary: Renders filterable sections of tasks grouped by section, with a create task button and live data refresh from Supabase.

nodes.name: TaskRow.tsx
nodes.summary: Compact table-row component for a single task showing status badge, task name, deadline, and assignee initials with click-to-cycle-status.

nodes.name: TaskRow
nodes.summary: Renders a single task as a table row with status cycling on status badge click, formatted deadline, and assignee initials badge.

nodes.name: TimelineView.tsx
nodes.summary: SVG-based Gantt-style timeline component that renders task bars across date columns grouped by section, with zoom controls, month/day headers, status-colored bars, hover tooltips, and click-to-open-drawer.

nodes.name: TimelineView
nodes.summary: Computes timeline geometry from task deadlines and section groupings, renders an interactive SVG Gantt chart with zoom, tooltips, and task click delegation.

nodes.name: ResizableDivider.tsx
nodes.summary: Drag handle component that fires an onResize callback with pixel deltas as the user drags, supporting both horizontal and vertical resize directions.

nodes.name: ResizableDivider
nodes.summary: Attaches mousemove/mouseup listeners on drag start to compute movement delta and report it via onResize, cleaning up listeners on release.

nodes.name: avatar.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable avatar UI primitives including Avatar, AvatarImage, AvatarFallback, AvatarBadge, AvatarGroup, and AvatarGroupCount components built with cva-style class merging.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports six related sub-components from a single file following a compound-component pattern.

nodes.name: badge.tsx
nodes.summary: Polymorphic badge UI component with variant-based styling using cva, supporting default, secondary, destructive, and outline variants.

nodes.name: WorkspaceData.tsx
nodes.summary: Data-provider component that manages live sections and tasks state, handles URL-param-triggered AI brief state, and renders children as a render-prop pattern.

nodes.name: WorkspaceData
nodes.summary: Fetches and holds live sections and tasks from Supabase, detects a pending AI brief from URL params or localStorage, and passes all state to children via render props.

nodes.name: WorkspaceLayout.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level workspace layout orchestrating the graph/list/timeline/docs views, a resizable sidebar, AI chat panel, task drawer, and project header with delete action.

nodes.name: WorkspaceLayout
nodes.summary: Renders the full workspace UI with a view switcher, resizable sidebar, AI chat drawer, task drawer, and all subordinate feature components, managing all layout state.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Browser-side Supabase client factory that wraps createBrowserClient with the project's environment-variable URL and anon key.

nodes.name: createClient
nodes.summary: Returns a Supabase browser client configured from NEXT_PUBLIC environment variables.

nodes.name: graphStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing React Flow graph state including nodes, edges, and graph actions such as buildFromData, setNodes, removeTaskNode, and helpers for initializing graph layout from project data.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zustand's create pattern; the exported useGraphStore hook is the sole access point for all graph state mutations.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Central TypeScript type definitions for the entire application, covering Project, Task, Section, Member, ChecklistItem, and document-related interfaces, plus a utility function for computing RAG chunk relevance scores.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js login page that renders the LoginForm component centered on a full-screen authentication layout.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js registration page that renders the RegisterForm component centered on a full-screen authentication layout.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server-side dashboard page that fetches the user's project memberships with checklist progress from Supabase and renders a grid of ProjectCard components with completion statistics.

nodes.name: DashboardPage
nodes.summary: Async server component that authenticates the user, queries project memberships and done tasks, computes per-project checklist completion counts, and renders the dashboard UI.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Dynamic invite page that validates a project invite token from Supabase, checks membership status, and renders an accept-invite form or redirects if the token is invalid or user is already a member.

nodes.name: InvitePage
nodes.summary: Async server component that looks up the invite token, checks for expiry and existing membership, and renders a confirmation UI with an accept server action.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Protected new-project page that verifies authentication via Supabase and renders the CreateProjectForm for the authenticated user.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side settings page allowing users to update their display name and Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) API key stored in the Supabase profiles table.

nodes.name: SettingsPage
nodes.summary: Client component with form state for name and BYOK key, loads profile data from Supabase on mount, and saves updates via the Supabase profiles table.

nodes.name: LoginForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Authentication form component supporting email/password sign-in and Google OAuth via Supabase, with loading state and redirect on success.

nodes.name: LoginForm
nodes.summary: Client component providing email/password login and Google OAuth using Supabase auth, showing error toasts and redirecting to the requested or default route on success.

nodes.name: RegisterForm.tsx
nodes.summary: User registration form component that signs up a new user via Supabase, auto-signs them in, and redirects to the dashboard.

nodes.name: RegisterForm
nodes.summary: Client component collecting name, email, and password to register and immediately sign in a new user via Supabase, then navigate to the dashboard.

nodes.name: ProjectCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card component that displays a project's name, deadline countdown, member avatars, and checklist completion progress.

nodes.name: ProjectCard
nodes.summary: Presentational component rendering a card with project metadata including deadline, formatted date, member avatar stack, and a progress bar for checklist completion.

nodes.name: CreateProjectForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Project creation form using React Hook Form with Zod validation that submits to an API route, stores any brief in localStorage, and navigates to the new project page.

nodes.name: CreateProjectForm
nodes.summary: Client component with React Hook Form + Zod validation that posts project creation data to an API route, persists a brief to localStorage, and redirects on success.

nodes.name: MemberAvatarStack.tsx
nodes.summary: Compact avatar stack component that renders up to a configurable maximum number of member avatars with initials fallback and an overflow count badge.

nodes.name: MemberAvatarStack
nodes.summary: Renders a stacked row of Avatar components for visible members and shows a numeric overflow indicator when the member list exceeds the max.

nodes.name: CreateSectionDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Dialog component for creating a new section in a project, inserting the record into Supabase and triggering a callback on success.

nodes.name: CreateSectionDialog
nodes.summary: Client component that opens a modal dialog to capture a section name, inserts it into Supabase with project_id, and invokes the onCreated callback on success.

nodes.name: CreateTaskDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Dialog component for creating a new task within a project section, capturing name, section, type, and optional deadline before inserting into Supabase.

nodes.name: CreateTaskDialog
nodes.summary: Client component presenting a modal form to create a task with configurable fields (name, section, type, deadline), inserts via Supabase, and invokes onCreated callback.

nodes.name: button.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable Button component built with class-variance-authority providing multiple visual variants and sizes via Tailwind CSS.

nodes.name: card.tsx
nodes.summary: Card UI primitive providing Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardAction, CardContent, and CardFooter layout components.

nodes.name: dialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Radix UI Dialog primitives wrapped with Tailwind styling, providing Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogFooter, DialogTitle, and DialogDescription components.

nodes.name: dropdown-menu.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Radix UI DropdownMenu primitive wrappers including items, sub-menus, checkbox items, radio groups, separators, and keyboard shortcuts.

nodes.name: input.tsx
nodes.summary: Styled input element component wrapping the native HTML input with consistent Tailwind CSS styling.

nodes.name: label.tsx
nodes.summary: Styled label component built on Radix Label primitive with Tailwind CSS typography styling.

nodes.name: progress.tsx
nodes.summary: Composable progress indicator UI providing Progress, ProgressTrack, ProgressIndicator, ProgressLabel, and ProgressValue sub-components.

nodes.name: select.tsx
nodes.summary: Radix UI Select primitives wrapped with Tailwind styling, providing a complete accessible select widget with trigger, content, items, scroll buttons, and separators.

nodes.name: separator.tsx
nodes.summary: Styled horizontal or vertical separator component built on Radix Separator primitive.

nodes.name: sheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Radix UI Dialog-based Sheet (slide-over panel) component supporting configurable side positioning with header, footer, title, and description sub-components.

nodes.name: skeleton.tsx
nodes.summary: Skeleton loading placeholder component using Tailwind pulse animation for content loading states.

nodes.name: tabs.tsx
nodes.summary: Radix UI Tabs primitives wrapped with Tailwind styling, supporting horizontal and vertical orientation with variant-based list styling.

nodes.name: textarea.tsx
nodes.summary: Styled textarea element component with consistent Tailwind CSS sizing and focus ring styling.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Shared utility helpers providing class merging (cn), date formatting, days-until countdown, and name-initials extraction used across the entire UI layer.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler that orchestrates the AI chat pipeline: authenticates the user, resolves provider API keys (BYOK), constructs the system prompt and project context, streams AI responses via the Vercel AI SDK, and emits ghost previews for pending write tool calls.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles POST requests to the chat endpoint: verifies auth, decodes BYOK API keys, builds the AI model instance and system prompt, runs streamText with tool support, and returns a streaming UI message response.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route for managing Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) API keys: GET returns which providers have saved keys, POST saves or deletes a key for a given provider, storing keys base64-encoded in the user's profile.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Returns a map of provider IDs to booleans indicating whether the authenticated user has a saved API key for each provider.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Saves or deletes a BYOK API key for a specific provider, base64-encoding the key before persisting it to the user's Supabase profile.

nodes.name: ActionPreviewCard.tsx
nodes.summary: React component that renders a preview card for pending AI-proposed graph mutations, allowing the user to commit (apply) or discard them before they are persisted.

nodes.name: ActionPreviewCard
nodes.summary: Displays a list of proposed AI tool-call changes with commit and discard controls; calls the execute-tools endpoint on commit and invokes the onCommit callback on success.

nodes.name: ChatInput.tsx
nodes.summary: Controlled chat input component composing a textarea, file-attach button, and provider dropdown into a unified message-entry UI with reply-to and file-attachment indicators.

nodes.name: ChatInput
nodes.summary: Renders the chat input bar including the Textarea, ProviderDropdown, FileAttachButton, reply-to strip, and a Send button, delegating all state changes upward via callback props.

nodes.name: ChatMessages.tsx
nodes.summary: Scrollable message list component that renders each chat message, a ghost preview card for pending AI actions, and auto-scrolls to the bottom on new messages.

nodes.name: ChatMessages
nodes.summary: Maps over the message list to render Message components, conditionally renders the ActionPreviewCard for ghost previews, and maintains auto-scroll behavior.

nodes.name: ChatPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level chat panel component that orchestrates the chat store, AI streaming hook, message submission, ghost preview lifecycle, and composes ChatMessages and ChatInput.

nodes.name: ChatPanel
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full chat interaction: manages input state, triggers message sending via the AI hook, builds ghost nodes from streamed tool calls, and wires commit/discard callbacks.

nodes.name: FileAttachButton.tsx
nodes.summary: Button component for attaching files to chat messages; handles plain-text files directly and uploads binary/PDF files via the document extraction API, then passes the extracted text upward.

nodes.name: FileAttachButton
nodes.summary: Renders a hidden file input triggered by a paperclip button; reads text files directly or uploads to the document extract API and calls onExtracted with the resulting text.

nodes.name: Message.tsx
nodes.summary: Individual chat message bubble component rendering user and assistant messages with reply-to context, role-based styling, and a reply action button.

nodes.name: Message
nodes.summary: Renders a single chat message with role-based alignment and colors, an optional reply-to preview strip, and an on-hover reply button.

nodes.name: ProviderDropdown.tsx
nodes.summary: Dropdown component for selecting an AI provider; loads saved BYOK key status on mount, allows users to switch providers, and provides an inline form to save a new API key if none is stored.

nodes.name: ProviderDropdown
nodes.summary: Manages an open/closed dropdown with multi-step flow: lists providers, shows a key-input form for providers missing a BYOK key, saves/deletes keys via the BYOK API, and reports the selected provider upward.

nodes.name: ReplyBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Small UI strip showing a truncated preview of the message being replied to, with a dismiss button to clear the reply-to state.

nodes.name: ReplyBar
nodes.summary: Renders the reply-to preview bar with a truncated message content snippet and a close button that calls the onClear callback.

nodes.name: context.ts
nodes.summary: Server-side utility that fetches full project state from Supabase (project metadata, members, tasks, sections, checklist items) and assembles a structured context object used to ground AI system prompts.

nodes.name: buildProjectContext
nodes.summary: Fetches project, member, task, section, and checklist data in parallel from Supabase and returns a rich context object with computed fields such as days remaining, task stats, and checklist summaries.

nodes.name: ghostBuilder.ts
nodes.summary: Utility that converts AI write-tool call payloads into temporary ghost React Flow nodes and edges, allowing users to visually preview proposed graph changes before committing them.

nodes.name: buildGhostNodesFromToolCalls
nodes.summary: Iterates over AI tool call payloads and maps create_task and create_member operations to ghost ReactFlow node objects, using the project context to resolve positions.

nodes.name: preview.ts
nodes.summary: Transforms AI write-tool call arrays into a structured GhostPreview object summarising the proposed changes as human-readable descriptions for display in the ActionPreviewCard.

nodes.name: buildGhostPreview
nodes.summary: Maps an array of tool calls to GhostPreview change descriptors, computing a human-readable summary of the fields that will be written.

nodes.name: prompts.ts
nodes.summary: Builds the AI system prompt string from project context and user identity, embedding member lists, checklist summaries, and provider-specific tool-usage rules.

nodes.name: buildSystemPrompt
nodes.summary: Constructs the full system prompt by injecting project context, current user identity, checklist status, and provider-specific instructions into a structured template string.

nodes.name: providers.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the PROVIDERS registry of supported AI providers (Anthropic, Groq, OpenAI, Google, xAI) with their display metadata, and exports a factory function to instantiate the correct model SDK given a provider ID and API key.

nodes.name: getModelInstance
nodes.summary: Returns a provider-specific AI SDK model instance by mapping a ProviderId and API key to the corresponding createAnthropic/createGroq/createOpenAI/createGoogleGenerativeAI/createXai call.

nodes.name: definitions.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all AI tool schemas (search, get_project_context, create/update/delete task, create member, add/complete/remove checklist item, bulk_create_tasks) using Zod and the Vercel AI SDK tool helper, wiring each to the tool dispatcher.

nodes.name: buildTools
nodes.summary: Constructs and returns the full tool map (12+ tools) for the AI streaming pipeline, each with a Zod input schema, description, and execute handler that delegates to the tool dispatcher.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-exporting buildTools, executeToolCall, executeToolCalls, WRITE_TOOLS, and ToolResult from the tools sub-package.

nodes.name: messageUtils.ts
nodes.summary: Utility helpers for chat message objects: extracts plain-text content from multi-part Vercel AI SDK messages and checks whether a tool name belongs to the write-tool set.

nodes.name: getMessageText
nodes.summary: Extracts text content from a Vercel AI SDK UIMessage by iterating over parts (for newer format) or content array (for legacy format).

nodes.name: chatStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing chat UI state: the selected AI provider, reply-to message, attached file, pending tool calls, and ghost preview data shared across the chat component tree.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler that accepts multipart form uploads, stores files in Supabase Storage, inserts document records, extracts full text from PDF/DOCX/TXT files, chunks the text, generates embeddings, and persists document chunks for RAG retrieval.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles POST requests to upload a project document: authenticates the user, validates project membership, uploads the file to storage, parses its text content, and asynchronously generates and stores vector embeddings.

nodes.name: activity-log.ts
nodes.summary: Provides functions to build human-readable summaries of AI tool calls and to index those summaries as searchable document chunks in the project's activity log, enabling RAG-based querying of past AI actions.

nodes.name: buildActivitySummary
nodes.summary: Converts an array of AI tool call records and project context into a structured markdown-style summary string describing what actions were taken in an AI session.

nodes.name: indexActivity
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full activity indexing pipeline: builds a summary, chunks it, embeds the chunks, and upserts them as document_chunks rows along with tool call metadata for future RAG retrieval.

nodes.name: chunker.ts
nodes.summary: Provides text chunking and document sub-type detection utilities used by both the upload pipeline and the activity log indexer to prepare text for vector embedding.

nodes.name: chunkText
nodes.summary: Splits a text string into overlapping chunks of configurable size, normalizing whitespace and filtering out chunks below a minimum length threshold.

nodes.name: detectDocSubType
nodes.summary: Heuristically classifies a document as a rubric, assignment, lecture note, or general resource based on its filename and text content.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Centralizes numeric and configuration constants for the AI layer including chunk sizing parameters, retrieval top-K, task positioning offsets, hybrid search weights, and section color palettes.

nodes.name: embed.ts
nodes.summary: Wraps the Hugging Face Transformers.js pipeline to provide lazy-initialized text embedding functions used throughout the RAG pipeline for both document indexing and query embedding.

nodes.name: embedTexts
nodes.summary: Batches an array of text strings through the embedding model and returns a corresponding array of float32 embedding vectors.

nodes.name: embedQuery
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that embeds a single query string and returns its vector, used at search time in the retrieval pipeline.

nodes.name: retrieval.ts
nodes.summary: Implements vector similarity search and hybrid (vector + full-text) search over project document chunks via Supabase RPC calls, serving as the core retrieval layer for the RAG pipeline.

nodes.name: searchDocuments
nodes.summary: Embeds a query string and performs a pure vector similarity search against project document chunks, optionally filtered by document type, returning ranked RetrievedChunk results.

nodes.name: hybridSearchChunks
nodes.summary: Performs a hybrid search combining vector similarity and full-text ranking via a Supabase RPC function, applying configurable vector and text weights and optional doc-type filtering.

nodes.name: checklist.ts
nodes.summary: AI tool handlers for checklist operations: creating new checklist items within a project and linking existing tasks to a checklist item via Supabase.

nodes.name: handleAddChecklistItem
nodes.summary: Inserts a new checklist item into the project, auto-ordering it after existing items by querying the current count first.

nodes.name: handleLinkTaskToItem
nodes.summary: Updates a task record to associate it with a checklist item by setting the checklist_item_id foreign key.

nodes.name: dependency.ts
nodes.summary: AI tool handlers for setting and removing task blocking dependencies by updating the blocked_by_id field on task records in Supabase.

nodes.name: handleSetDependency
nodes.summary: Sets the blocked_by_id on a task to establish a blocking dependency relationship between two tasks.

nodes.name: handleRemoveDependency
nodes.summary: Clears the blocked_by_id on a task to remove an existing blocking dependency.

nodes.name: dispatcher.ts
nodes.summary: Central dispatcher that routes AI tool call objects to their respective handler functions across all tool modules, executing them sequentially or in parallel and collecting results.

nodes.name: executeToolCall
nodes.summary: Routes a single AI tool call to the matching handler function based on the tool name, passing project context and Supabase client through.

nodes.name: executeToolCalls
nodes.summary: Iterates over an array of AI tool call objects and dispatches each one, collecting results and errors for return to the AI layer.

nodes.name: project.ts
nodes.summary: AI tool read handlers for project-level queries: fetching project context, reading individual tasks with relations, computing member workload distribution, and listing tasks by section.

nodes.name: handleReadProject
nodes.summary: Delegates to buildProjectContext to return a full project context object for the given project ID.

nodes.name: handleReadTask
nodes.summary: Fetches a single task with its assignee profile, claims, and associated documents using Supabase nested select.

nodes.name: handleReadMemberLoad
nodes.summary: Computes workload statistics per project member by joining tasks and project_members tables and counting open versus completed tasks for each member.

nodes.name: handleReadTasksBySection
nodes.summary: Queries tasks for a project optionally filtered by section ID or status, returning task details with assignee and section relation data.

nodes.name: search.ts
nodes.summary: AI tool handler for document search that combines hybrid and pure vector search results, deduplicates them, and formats them into a scored context string for the AI model.

nodes.name: handleSearchDocuments
nodes.summary: Runs both hybrid and vector search queries, merges and deduplicates the results by chunk ID, sorts by score, and formats the top results as a numbered list with scores and document labels.

nodes.name: section.ts
nodes.summary: AI tool handler for adding a new section to a project, resolving a section color from the palette and auto-ordering the section after existing ones.

nodes.name: handleAddSection
nodes.summary: Inserts a new section into the project using a cyclic color from SECTION_COLORS and auto-ordered position based on existing section count.

nodes.name: sectionResolver.ts
nodes.summary: Utility that resolves a section ID for task placement: returns a passed-in ID directly or performs a case-insensitive fuzzy lookup by section name in the project.

nodes.name: resolveSectionId
nodes.summary: Returns the provided sectionId if non-null; otherwise performs an ILIKE query against section names within the project and returns the matched section's ID.

nodes.name: task.ts
nodes.summary: AI tool handlers for full CRUD operations on tasks: adding with section resolution and position calculation, updating fields, deleting, and batch-assigning tasks to project members.

nodes.name: handleAddTask
nodes.summary: Creates a new task record with section resolution, position calculation based on existing task count, and support for all task fields including assignee, deadline, and dependencies.

nodes.name: handleUpdateTask
nodes.summary: Updates arbitrary fields on an existing task record identified by task_id, returning the updated task.

nodes.name: handleDeleteTask
nodes.summary: Deletes a task record by its ID using Supabase delete query.

nodes.name: handleAssignTasksBatch
nodes.summary: Accepts an array of task-assignee pairs and concurrently updates each task's assignee_id, collecting and reporting any per-task errors.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the shared TypeScript type for AI tool call inputs used across all tool handler modules in the lib/ai/tools layer.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler that creates a new project, inserts default sections, and adds the creator as an owner-role project member in Supabase, requiring authenticated access.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles POST requests to create a project record, seed default sections, and register the requester as owner — returns the new project or an error response.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler that removes a specified document from the Supabase Storage 'project-docs' bucket after verifying the caller is authenticated.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates the user, then deletes a document file from Supabase Storage by path, returning success or an error response.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler that deletes a project record from Supabase, enforcing ownership by checking the caller's role in project_members before proceeding.

nodes.name: DELETE
nodes.summary: Verifies the authenticated user holds the 'owner' role on the target project, then issues a service-level delete against the projects table.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler that downloads a document from Supabase Storage and extracts its plain text using pdf-parse for PDFs, mammoth for DOCX files, or a plain text fallback, returning up to 50,000 characters.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Downloads a file from storage, detects its type (pdf/docx/text), dynamically imports the appropriate parser library, and returns the extracted text content truncated to 50k characters.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dynamic import() calls to lazily load pdf-parse and mammoth, avoiding server-side bundle issues with binary parsers in Next.js.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Root page component that checks Supabase session state and redirects authenticated users to /dashboard or unauthenticated users to /login.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Server-side Supabase client factory that creates a cookie-aware Supabase client for use in Next.js Server Components and API routes, handling cookie get/set for session persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @supabase/ssr's createServerClient with Next.js cookies() to bridge Supabase auth session into the server request lifecycle.

nodes.name: createClient
nodes.summary: Instantiates and returns a Supabase server client configured to read and write cookies from the current Next.js request context for session management.

nodes.name: 001_init.sql
nodes.summary: Foundation migration establishing the core relational schema for grouply: user profiles, projects, project membership, task management (sections, tasks, claims, documents, history), invite tokens, and comprehensive Row Level Security policies with helper functions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Supabase auth integration (auth.users, auth.uid()), RLS helper functions (is_project_member, is_project_owner) with SECURITY DEFINER, pgvector-compatible setup, and Realtime publication for select tables.

nodes.name: profiles
nodes.summary: User profile table linked to Supabase auth.users, storing display name, avatar URL, and a legacy BYOK key field. Auto-populated on signup via trigger.

nodes.name: projects
nodes.summary: Core project entity storing name, subject, description, deadline, and owner reference. RLS restricts access to project members only.

nodes.name: project_members
nodes.summary: Many-to-many join table between projects and profiles with a role field (owner/member), enforcing unique project-user pairs.

nodes.name: sections
nodes.summary: Project-scoped organizational sections (e.g., kanban columns) with display color and ordering.

nodes.name: checklist_items
nodes.summary: Project-level checklist items with ordering, used for structured task templates or deliverable tracking.

nodes.name: tasks
nodes.summary: Central task entity with status workflow (todo/doing/review/done/blocked), type classification, graph position (pos_x/pos_y), self-referential blocking dependency, section/checklist assignment, and auto-updated timestamps.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-referential blocked_by_id FK and pos_x/pos_y columns support the graph visualization feature.

nodes.name: task_claims
nodes.summary: Records which users have claimed (volunteered for) a task; enforces unique task-user pairs.

nodes.name: task_documents
nodes.summary: Stores URL references to documents attached to individual tasks, with uploader tracking.

nodes.name: task_history
nodes.summary: Audit log for task changes, capturing action type with old and new values as JSONB for flexible diff storage.

nodes.name: project_invites
nodes.summary: Token-based project invite links with optional expiry, allowing owners to invite new members via a shareable URL.

nodes.name: 002_project_documents.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the project_documents table for storing file upload metadata (path, URL, type), enabling the pgvector extension, and applying RLS policies for member access.
nodes.languageNotes: Enables the pgvector extension as a prerequisite for the document_chunks embedding table in migration 003.

nodes.name: project_documents
nodes.summary: Stores metadata for files uploaded to a project (name, storage path, public URL, file type, uploader), used as the parent for chunked embeddings.

nodes.name: 003_document_chunks.sql
nodes.summary: Migration creating the document_chunks table for storing 384-dimensional embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) with an IVFFlat cosine index, plus an initial vector similarity search RPC upgraded in migration 004.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pgvector's ivfflat index with vector_cosine_ops and lists=100 for approximate nearest-neighbor search on 384-dim embeddings.

nodes.name: document_chunks
nodes.summary: Stores text chunks from project documents along with their vector embeddings for semantic similarity search; linked to both the parent project and source document.

nodes.name: 004_rag_v2.sql
nodes.summary: RAG v2 migration upgrading document_chunks with doc_type classification, auto-generated full-text search tsvector column, metadata JSONB, and two search RPCs: upgraded match_document_chunks and a new hybrid_search_chunks combining vector and keyword scoring.
nodes.languageNotes: hybrid_search_chunks uses a weighted linear combination of cosine similarity and ts_rank scores (default 0.7/0.3 split), with a 4x oversample in the vector pool before re-ranking. Uses 'simple' dictionary to support non-English (Vietnamese) text.

nodes.name: 005_byok_keys.sql
nodes.summary: Single-statement migration adding a byok_keys JSONB column to the profiles table to store per-user Bring Your Own Key API credentials.

nodes.name: run_all_pending.sql
nodes.summary: Idempotent consolidated script combining migrations 002 through 004 for safe re-execution in the Supabase SQL Editor, using IF NOT EXISTS guards and DO blocks for policy checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Designed to be run multiple times safely — wraps CREATE POLICY statements in DO $$ BEGIN ... IF NOT EXISTS ... END $$ blocks to avoid duplicate policy errors.

nodes.name: project_documents
nodes.summary: Idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS definition for project_documents as part of the consolidated run-all script.

nodes.name: document_chunks
nodes.summary: Idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS definition for document_chunks as part of the consolidated run-all script.

nodes.name: .env.local.example
nodes.summary: Template listing the required environment variables for local development, including Supabase connection credentials and any AI provider API keys.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agentic coding guidelines warning that this project uses a non-standard Next.js version with breaking API changes, directing contributors to read the bundled docs before writing code.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code project instructions file that references AGENTS.md via an @-import directive, forwarding all coding guidelines to that file.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for Grouply covering AI-powered context understanding, multi-mode workspace, key features, AI tools, tech stack (Next.js 16, Supabase, Tailwind, Zustand), and license.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui configuration specifying the component style, Tailwind CSS integration, RSC/TSX settings, path aliases, icon library, and component registry for the project's UI component toolchain.
nodes.languageNotes: Used by the shadcn/ui CLI to scaffold and manage UI components; the aliases field maps to tsconfig path aliases.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root package manifest defining project metadata, npm scripts (dev, build, start, lint), all runtime dependencies (Next.js 16, React 19, Supabase, AI SDK, Zustand, Radix UI), and dev dependencies.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode, Next.js plugin integration, path alias '@/*' mapped to the project root, and bundler module resolution for the monorepo.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses 'moduleResolution: bundler' and 'paths' aliases that are mirrored in components.json for shadcn/ui tooling.

nodes.name: ARCHITECTURE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive system architecture reference for Grouply covering tech stack, database schema, AI agentic loop, RAG system, Zustand state management, Supabase realtime subscriptions, authentication, API routes, and component architecture across 52 sections.

nodes.name: GROUPLY_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step implementation guide for enhancing the Grouply RAG system with a replan memory store, metadata-aware chunking, hybrid vector and full-text search, new AI tools, and automatic replan event indexing on commit.

nodes.name: GROUPLY_RAG_V2.md
nodes.summary: Second-generation RAG implementation guide introducing an activity-log virtual document pattern, document type classification, hybrid search retrieval upgrades, upgraded AI tool definitions, and auto-indexing of AI commit activity for long-term memory.

nodes.name: 2026-05-14-sprint1-auth-dashboard-crud.md
nodes.summary: Sprint 1 implementation plan covering project setup, TypeScript types, Supabase migrations, authentication pages, dashboard, task CRUD, workspace shell/list view, invite flow, and settings page — forming the foundational data and UI layer of the application.

nodes.name: 2026-05-14-sprint2-graph-reactflow.md
nodes.summary: Sprint 2 implementation plan for the interactive ReactFlow-based task graph, including Zustand graph store, custom node components (SectionNode, TaskNode, GhostTaskNode), dependency edge rendering, toolbar, and main canvas integration into the workspace page.

nodes.name: 2026-05-14-sprint3-ai-chat-agent.md
nodes.summary: Sprint 3 implementation plan for the AI chat agent, covering Zustand chatStore, AI context builder, system prompt builder, tool definitions and executor, simulate mode, streaming API route, ghost task builder, chat UI components, workspace integration, and project brief parsing.

nodes.name: 2026-05-14-sprint4-claim-checklist-contribution.md
nodes.summary: Sprint 4 implementation plan for task ownership and tracking features: task drawer, ClaimBadge, checklist sidebar, contribution bar, and their integration into the WorkspaceClient, including a full MVP verification checklist.

nodes.name: 2026-05-16-ai-chat-v2.md
nodes.summary: AI Chat v2 implementation plan adding an agentic loop with SSE streaming, lazy context loading, file attachment (PDF/DOCX/TXT extraction), reply threading UI, and a full rewrite of ChatPanel to support these capabilities.

nodes.name: 2026-05-18-prompt-redesign.md
nodes.summary: Focused plan to restructure the AI system prompt builder in lib/ai/prompts.ts, add English tool description overrides for Groq, and thread a provider parameter through the chat API route for provider-aware prompt generation.

nodes.name: 2026-05-27-grouply-clean-refactor.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive surgical refactor plan addressing AI constants centralization, discriminated union types, retrieval/chunker fixes, decomposition of the monolithic tools file into a domain-based lib/ai/tools/ directory, shrinking ChatPanel.tsx into focused sub-components, and splitting WorkspaceClient into data and layout layers.

nodes.name: 2026-05-29-provider-selector.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for adding a multi-provider AI selector and BYOK (bring-your-own-key) API key UI, including a new providers.ts abstraction, Supabase byok_keys migration, BYOK API route, ProviderDropdown component, chatStore updates, and removal of the simulate mode.

nodes.name: 2026-05-14-grouply-mvp-design.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive MVP design document for Grouply v1.0 covering architecture, Supabase database schema, auth, pages and routes, ReactFlow graph visualization, AI chat agent with 12 tools, task management, and sprint planning across 13 major sections.

nodes.name: 2026-05-18-prompt-redesign.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for restructuring the AI prompt system, addressing problems with the existing approach and defining a 5-block system prompt structure, Groq-specific tool description overrides, and token budget constraints.

nodes.name: 2026-05-27-grouply-clean-refactor-design.md
nodes.summary: Surgical deep-refactor design document detailing a planned restructuring of the Grouply codebase, including file structure changes, type system updates, component splits for WorkspaceClient and ChatPanel, dead code removal, and success criteria.

nodes.name: 2026-05-29-provider-selector-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for the provider selector and BYOK (bring-your-own-key) API key UI, covering provider configuration, database schema changes, chat header UI, a new /api/user/byok route, store changes, and removal of simulate mode.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore pattern file for the understand-anything analysis tool, listing directories and file patterns to exclude from codebase scanning (e.g. node_modules, .next, generated files).

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet that imports Tailwind CSS, tw-animate-css, and shadcn design tokens, then defines the full light and dark mode CSS custom property palette (colors, radii, sidebar, chart tokens) used throughout the application.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Tailwind v4 @import syntax and @theme inline block to bridge CSS custom properties into Tailwind utility classes; colors are defined in the perceptually-uniform oklch color space.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Next.js layout that sets global metadata, applies the Inter font, wraps all pages in an HTML shell with the app's language set to Vietnamese, and mounts the global Toaster notification component.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Default export Next.js root layout component that renders the html/body shell with the Inter font class and mounts the Toaster component alongside all child pages.

nodes.name: sonner.tsx
nodes.summary: Themed wrapper around the sonner Toaster library, integrating next-themes for automatic light/dark mode, custom Lucide icon overrides for each toast type, and shadcn CSS variable bindings for visual consistency.

nodes.name: Toaster
nodes.summary: React component wrapping the sonner Toaster with theme awareness (via useTheme), custom icon set from Lucide React, and shadcn CSS custom property bindings for toast appearance.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint flat-config extending Next.js core-web-vitals and TypeScript rulesets, with explicit ignores for build output directories.

nodes.name: Grouply_Build_Spec.docx
nodes.summary: Build specification document describing the full feature set, architecture decisions, and implementation requirements for the Grouply collaborative project management application.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Next.js configuration file with an empty config object, serving as the project's Next.js configuration entry point with no custom overrides yet applied.

nodes.name: postcss.config.mjs
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling the @tailwindcss/postcss plugin for processing Tailwind CSS v4 utility classes during the build.

nodes.name: proxy.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js middleware proxy that initializes a Supabase server client for session cookie management and enforces authentication-based route protection, redirecting unauthenticated users to /login and authenticated users away from auth pages.

nodes.name: proxy
nodes.summary: Async middleware handler that creates a Supabase SSR client with cookie forwarding, verifies the current user session, and applies route-guard redirects to enforce authentication on protected paths.

layers.name: Pages & API Routes
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages for authentication, dashboard, workspace, and invite flows, plus server-side API route handlers for project CRUD, document management, AI chat, and BYOK key storage.

layers.name: UI Components
layers.description: All React feature components organized by domain (workspace, task, graph, chat, checklist, timeline, auth, dashboard) plus the shadcn/ui primitive component library used throughout the application.

layers.name: AI Service Layer
layers.description: All AI and RAG infrastructure including provider registry, prompt builders, context assembly, embedding generation, vector retrieval, document chunking, ghost task preview, and the full suite of AI tool handlers that the chat agent can invoke.

layers.name: State & Types
layers.description: Zustand client-side state stores managing React Flow graph state and AI chat session state, plus the shared TypeScript type definitions that are imported across the entire application.

layers.name: Backend Client
layers.description: Supabase client factories for both browser and server-side SSR usage, plus the Next.js middleware that enforces authentication guards on all protected routes.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Supabase SQL migrations defining the full PostgreSQL schema (profiles, projects, tasks, sections, claims, history, invites, documents, vector embeddings) and the logical table entities derived from those migrations.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Architecture references, RAG implementation guides, sprint plans, feature specs, and the original build specification covering all phases of Grouply's development.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project configuration files governing the build toolchain, TypeScript compilation, ESLint rules, PostCSS/Tailwind, shadcn component settings, and environment variable templates.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what Grouply is: a collaborative project management app combining real-time task tracking, a React Flow dependency graph, a Gantt timeline, and AI-powered task management with RAG document search. Reading this first gives you the mental model needed before diving into any code — you will see the four major feature pillars (workspace, graph, chat, RAG) that the rest of the tour unpacks layer by layer.

tour.title: Architecture Reference
tour.description: The architecture document maps every system layer — pages, components, AI service, state stores, backend client, and data — and how they interconnect. It is the single reference that explains why the codebase is structured the way it is, and it will give you the vocabulary to understand each subsequent tour stop. Read it alongside the README to build a complete picture before touching any code.

tour.title: Auth & Routing Guards
tour.description: Before a user ever sees the workspace, two mechanisms gate access. The SSR middleware in `proxy.ts` runs on every request and redirects unauthenticated users to login, using the server-side Supabase client (`lib/supabase/server.ts`) to read the session from cookies. Together these two files form the security boundary that all subsequent pages depend on — every API route and server page imports `server.ts` to verify identity.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js middleware (the file formerly called `middleware.ts`, here `proxy.ts`) runs in the Edge Runtime before any page or API route renders. Returning a `NextResponse.redirect()` from middleware is the canonical way to enforce auth guards without touching individual pages.

tour.title: Shared Type System
tour.description: With 22 fan-in edges, `types/index.ts` is the single most depended-upon file in the project — every feature component, store, and AI tool imports from it. It defines the core domain vocabulary: `Project`, `Task`, `Section`, `Member`, `ChecklistItem`, and more. Understanding these interfaces is prerequisite knowledge for every step that follows, because they are the contract that connects the database, the React components, and the AI tools.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript interface files used as a central 'domain vocabulary' are a common pattern in large React apps. Keeping all shared types in one barrel module prevents circular imports and makes refactoring (e.g., adding a field to Task) a single-file change that propagates everywhere via type checking.

tour.title: Auth & Dashboard Flow
tour.description: The user journey starts at the root page (`app/page.tsx`), which redirects authenticated users to the dashboard. The dashboard page (`app/dashboard/page.tsx`) uses the server-side Supabase client to fetch the user's projects server-side, then passes them to `ProjectCard` components. The login and register pages (`app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`, `app/(auth)/register/page.tsx`) delegate to thin form components that call Supabase Auth. This layer shows how Next.js App Router separates server data-fetching from client interactivity.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router distinguishes Server Components (no `'use client'` directive) from Client Components. Server Components run only on the server and can directly `await` database calls — no API round-trip needed. Client Components run in the browser and handle interactivity. Pages in the `app/` directory default to Server Components unless marked otherwise.

tour.title: Core Database Schema
tour.description: The foundation migration (`001_init.sql`) defines every core table that powers Grouply: `profiles`, `projects`, `project_members`, `sections`, `tasks`, `task_claims`, `task_history`, `checklist_items`, and `project_invites`. These tables are the source of truth for all the data you will see flowing through the components above. The `tasks` table is particularly important — it stores not just content fields but also graph position (`x`, `y`) so the React Flow canvas can restore node layout across sessions.
tour.languageLesson: Supabase uses Row Level Security (RLS) policies defined directly in SQL migrations. This means authorization logic lives in the database layer rather than only in application code — even if a client calls the database directly (e.g., via the browser Supabase client), RLS prevents users from reading data they do not own.

tour.title: Workspace Entry Point
tour.description: The workspace page (`app/project/[id]/page.tsx`) is the primary destination after login — it is the server component that authenticates the user, verifies they are a project member, fetches all initial project data, and hands it to `WorkspaceClient`. This is where server-side data loading ends and client-side interactivity begins. Understanding this handoff is key: everything loaded here becomes the initial props that `WorkspaceData` exposes via React context.
tour.languageLesson: The server/client component boundary in Next.js App Router is crossed by passing serializable props from a Server Component to a Client Component marked with `'use client'`. The Server Component (`page.tsx`) fetches data, serializes it, and the Client Component (`WorkspaceClient.tsx`) receives it as props and sets up a React context — this pattern avoids waterfalls by loading all data in a single server pass.

tour.title: Workspace Layout Hub
tour.description: With a fan-out of 12, `WorkspaceLayout.tsx` is the architectural hub of the entire UI — it imports and orchestrates every major view: the task list, task graph, timeline, checklist sidebar, documents tab, chat panel, and contribution bar. It also reads from both Zustand stores and manages the currently selected view tab. This file is the best single place to understand how Grouply's features are assembled into the workspace experience.
tour.languageLesson: Zustand stores (`graphStore`, `chatStore`) are accessed by importing the store hook directly inside any component tree — there is no provider wrapper required, unlike React context. This means `WorkspaceLayout` can read graph selection state and the chat panel can read message state without prop-drilling through the component tree.

tour.title: React Flow Task Graph
tour.description: The graph view (`TaskGraph.tsx`) is Grouply's flagship visual feature: it renders tasks and sections as interactive React Flow nodes, syncs positions back to Supabase in real-time, and uses Dagre (via `GraphToolbar`) for automatic layout. The three node types — `TaskNode`, `SectionNode`, and `GhostTaskNode` — each render different visual states, while `DependencyEdge` draws directional arrows between tasks. The Zustand `graphStore` holds all React Flow node/edge state so other components (like `WorkspaceLayout`) can read the current selection.
tour.languageLesson: React Flow requires node and edge arrays to be managed outside the component (e.g., in Zustand) rather than local state, because React Flow internally optimizes renders based on object identity. Storing nodes in Zustand also allows other parts of the app (like the AI tools) to add ghost nodes without going through a React event.

tour.title: Task List & Detail Drawer
tour.description: The list view (`TaskList.tsx`) presents the same tasks as the graph but in a familiar accordion layout, grouped by section via `SectionAccordion`. Clicking a row opens `TaskDrawer.tsx` — a side sheet that is arguably the richest component in the codebase, hosting task editing, status changes, `ClaimBadge` for ownership, `ActivityLog` for history, dependency management, and an embedded `ChatPanel` for per-task AI conversation. It is the convergence point of four different feature domains.

tour.title: Timeline & Contribution Views
tour.description: Grouply provides two additional analytical views of the same task data. `TimelineView.tsx` renders an SVG Gantt chart that uses task deadlines and date-range geometry to place bars on a zoomable horizontal axis — it consumes the same `Task` type from `types/index.ts` as every other component. `ContributionBar.tsx` aggregates completed tasks per member and renders a proportional horizontal bar, giving the team a visual read on workload distribution. Both components are stateless presenters: they receive data from WorkspaceLayout and render it.

tour.title: AI Chat Panel
tour.description: The `ChatPanel.tsx` composes `ChatMessages` and `ChatInput` into the full chat experience, backed by the `chatStore` Zustand store. `ChatInput` hosts the provider selector dropdown (OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, etc.) and the file attachment button for attaching project documents to a query. When the AI responds with a tool call, `ActionPreviewCard` renders a confirmation UI before the action executes — this is the safety gate between AI suggestions and data mutations.
tour.languageLesson: The Vercel AI SDK's `useChat` hook returns a `messages` array where each message can contain multiple 'parts': text parts, tool-call parts, and tool-result parts. `ChatMessages.tsx` iterates these parts to render different UI — text bubbles for prose responses, `ActionPreviewCard` for pending tool calls — all from a single unified message stream.

tour.title: AI Chat API & Tool System
tour.description: The server-side entry for AI is `app/api/ai/chat/route.ts`, which streams responses using the Vercel AI SDK. It wires together three things: the provider registry (`lib/ai/providers.ts`), the system prompt builder (`lib/ai/prompts.ts`), and the tool barrel (`lib/ai/tools/index.ts`). The tools barrel exports Zod-validated `definitions.ts` (the schema the LLM sees) and the `dispatcher.ts` (which routes tool calls to the correct handler: `task.ts`, `section.ts`, `checklist.ts`, `dependency.ts`, `search.ts`, or `project.ts`).
tour.languageLesson: Tool calling in the Vercel AI SDK works by providing Zod schemas alongside the model call. The SDK automatically converts Zod schemas to the JSON Schema format expected by LLM APIs and validates the model's returned tool arguments against those schemas before passing them to your handler — this prevents type errors from malformed AI output.

tour.title: RAG Pipeline
tour.description: Grouply's AI can answer questions grounded in uploaded project documents via a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline. When a document is uploaded, `app/api/project/upload-doc/route.ts` calls the `chunker` to split text into overlapping segments, then `embed.ts` generates OpenAI vector embeddings for each chunk and stores them in Supabase. At query time, `retrieval.ts` performs a hybrid search combining pgvector cosine similarity with Postgres full-text search, and `context.ts` assembles the top chunks into the AI system prompt.
tour.languageLesson: Hybrid search combines vector similarity (semantic — finds conceptually related content even with different words) with full-text search (keyword — finds exact term matches). Supabase's `pgvector` extension stores embeddings as vector columns and provides the `<=>` cosine distance operator. Hybrid search is more robust than either approach alone because it handles both paraphrase and exact-match queries.

tour.title: RAG Schema & Vector Store
tour.description: The final three migrations extend the core schema to support the RAG pipeline. `002_project_documents.sql` adds the `project_documents` table and enables the `pgvector` extension. `003_document_chunks.sql` adds `document_chunks` with a `vector(1536)` embedding column. `004_rag_v2.sql` upgrades retrieval with a hybrid search SQL function combining `match_documents` (vector) and `websearch_to_tsquery` (full-text). The `005_byok_keys.sql` migration adds a `byok_keys` JSONB column to `profiles` so users can store their own API keys for any of the supported providers.
tour.languageLesson: pgvector stores embeddings as fixed-dimension float arrays (`vector(1536)` for OpenAI's text-embedding-3-small). An HNSW or IVFFlat index on the vector column makes approximate nearest-neighbor search fast at scale. Storing embeddings in the same Postgres database as application data (rather than a separate vector store) simplifies architecture and allows SQL joins between chunks and the tasks/projects they belong to.
